item.name: Factset Request Returns Data for the First Trade (official and Unofficial) After the Specified Time

request.name: Factset Request Returns Data for the First Trade (official and Unofficial) After the Specified Time

query.description:
(Required) Requested symbol or security. The symbol can be a FactSet exchange symbol, CUSIP, or SEDOL. **NOTE**: Only one identifier can be requested per request.

**TRY IT OUT** - Choose One from - BABA-USA,WALMEX-MX,7203-TKS,VOD-LON,NPN-JSE,MABAX,NZF,SPY-USA,AGG-USA,GLD-USA,AOR-USA,MNA-USA,UUP-USA,SP50-SPX,ESX-STX,XAO-ASX,WD-MSX,NG00-USA,GC00-USA,CC00-USA,C00-USA,FC00-USA,ER00-USA,EURUSD-FX1,USDMXN-FX1,AUDJPY-FX1,EURCZK-FX1,USDILS-FX1,USDZAR-FX1,US10YY-TU1,FDS#190621C00145000-USA,FDS#190621P00145000-USA
Request Identification String. Can be used by the application to keep track of requests. The id is not used by this service, however, it is included in the XML response.
This is the start date for the Snapshot History. Requests should be made in the format YYYYMMDD Default is current business day. Field can be sd or date. TRY IT OUT Cannot use current day.
This is the start time for the snapshot history requested. Requests should be made in a **HHMMSSS** format.Range from 000000 - 235959. **TRY IT OUT** Cannot use current day
The format of the output file. TRY IT OUT Choose from JSON, CSV, CSV_NO_HEADER
Flag to include request id and key in the CSV output.
Decides what type of trades to return, default is all trades and quotes, alternatively charting can be selected which will filter out unofficial trades.

item.name: Factset Tick History Service Returns Trade Data and Associated Quotes for a Client Specified Interval and Time Period

request.name: Factset Tick History Service Returns Trade Data and Associated Quotes for a Client Specified Interval and Time Period

description.content:
When using an HTTP GET request, the seach criteria is sent via the query string in the URL. Please make sure that all requests contain a date within the past trailing year. Fields are added using ampersands, with enumerations seperated by commas.

**Try it Out** - references a sandbox environment to simulate live reponses. Available `id` is limited to BABA-USA,WALMEX-MX,7203-TKS,VOD-LON,NPN-JSE,MABAX,NZF,SPY-USA,AGG-USA,GLD-USA,AOR-USA,MNA-USA,UUP-USA,SP50-SPX,ESX-STX,XAO-ASX,WD-MSX,NG00-USA,GC00-USA,CC00-USA,C00-USA,FC00-USA,ER00-USA,EURUSD-FX1,USDMXN-FX1,AUDJPY-FX1,EURCZK-FX1,USDILS-FX1,USDZAR-FX1,US10YY-TU1,FDS#190621C00145000-USA,FDS#190621P00145000-USA. Current day data is not available.

query.description:
(Required) Requested symbol or security. The symbol can be a FactSet exchange symbol, CUSIP, or SEDOL. **NOTE**: Only one identifier can be requested per request.

**TRY IT OUT** - Choose One from - BABA-USA,WALMEX-MX,7203-TKS,VOD-LON,NPN-JSE,MABAX,NZF,SPY-USA,AGG-USA,GLD-USA,AOR-USA,MNA-USA,UUP-USA,SP50-SPX,ESX-STX,XAO-ASX,WD-MSX,NG00-USA,GC00-USA,CC00-USA,C00-USA,FC00-USA,ER00-USA,EURUSD-FX1,USDMXN-FX1,AUDJPY-FX1,EURCZK-FX1,USDILS-FX1,USDZAR-FX1,US10YY-TU1,FDS#190621C00145000-USA,FDS#190621P00145000-USA
Request Identification String. Can be used by the application to keep track of requests.  The id is not used by this service, however, it is included in the XML response.
The format of the output file. **TRY IT OUT** Choose from JSON, CSV, CSV_NO_HEADER
All available fields. This is a comma-seperated list (no spaces). See definitions for details. Default is all fields when left blank.
This is the start date for the Snapshot History. Requests should be made in the format **YYYYMMDD**. Default is current business day. Field can be sd or date. **TRY IT OUT** Cannot use current day.

This is the end date for teh Snapshot History. Requests should be made in the format **YYYYMMDD**. If the period requested is larger than Max. Days requested, results will be limited to the Max Days starting at sd/date. Defautl is sd or date. **TRY IT OUT** Cannot use current day.
Requested start time. This is the start time for the snapshot history requested. Requests should be made in a **HHMMSSS** format. Range from 000000 - 235959. **TRY IT OUT** Cannot use current day.
Requested end time. This is the end time for the snapshot history requested. Requests should be made in a **HHMMSSS** format. Range from 000000 - 235959. **TRY IT OUT** Cannot use current day.

Requested time interval. This is the interval the data is returned from the service. Possible values from 1 Second (1S) to 1 Hour (1H). A maximum of 10 Tick History Requests and a maximum of 100,000 rows of data may be made in any one-minute period. Additional subsctiption is available to access 20 requests/200,000 rows of data per minute. Contact your FactSet team for details.
Flag to include request id and key in the CSV output.
Decides what type of trades to return, default is all trades and quotes, alternatively charting can be selected which will filter out unofficial trades.

response.name: OK

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: basic

response.name: Access Denied. The user is not authorized to view the data requested. Contact FactSet Consulting Services for assistance.

FactSet's Tick History provides cost-effective access to consolidated
real-time and delayed global exchange data. Proprietary technology
normalizes data from over 200 global exchanges and across 19 tick history
fields. Asset types integrated_

Contact Support:
 Name: FactSet Research Systems
 Email: api@factset.com
